creator: Lauren Forristal
title: (No AI) Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
content: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.
contentSnippet: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.

creator: Kirsten Korosec, Russell Brandom
title: (No AI) SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Russell Brandom
content: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.
contentSnippet: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

title: (No AI) All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
author: Richard Lawler
content:
![Anthropic logo on an orange background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STK269_ANTHROPIC_2_D.webp?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Anthropic was already navigating one dispute with the government [in its standoff with the Pentagon](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/886082/ai-vs-the-pentagon-killer-robots-mass-surveillance-and-red-lines), and then came an order on June 12th to block off foreign access to its most recently released AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. When they [launched](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5) on June 9th, Anthropic said “Fable 5’s capabilities exceed those of any model we’ve ever made generally available,” and that Claude Mythos 5 had the same underlying model, “but with the safeguards lifted in some areas.”

According to [reports](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949601/amazon-anthropic-fablemythos-government-ban), the order [came after](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/amazon-ceos-talks-with-u-s-officials-triggered-crackdown-on-anthropic-models-dcc90578?mod=hp_lead_pos1) conversations between Amazon and the White House about researchers saying they found ways to get Fable 5 to serve information that could be used in cyberattacks.

Anthropic responded by shutting access to both models for all customers, [saying](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access), “We are complying with the government’s legal directive and are removing access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all users. However, we disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people.”

_Read along below for all of the latest updates_

*   [Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949986/anthropic-fable-mythos-shutdown-sovereign-ai)
*   [China may have accessed Mythos](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949644/china-white-house-anthropic-mythos)
*   [Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949601/amazon-anthropic-fablemythos-government-ban)
*   [Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949553/anthropic-fable-5-mythos-5-government-national-security)
*   [Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable](https://www.theverge.com/news/946725/anthropic-releases-claude-fable-5-mythos)
contentSnippet:
Anthropic was already navigating one dispute with the government in its standoff with the Pentagon, and then came an order on June 12th to block off foreign access to its most recently released AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. When they launched on June 9th, Anthropic said “Fable 5’s capabilities exceed those of any model we’ve ever made generally available,” and that Claude Mythos 5 had the same underlying model, “but with the safeguards lifted in some areas.”
According to reports, the order came after conversations between Amazon and the White House about researchers saying they found ways to get Fable 5 to serve information that could be used in cyberattacks. 
Anthropic responded by shutting access to both models for all customers, saying, “We are complying with the government’s legal directive and are removing access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all users. However, we disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people.”
Read along below for all of the latest updates

China may have accessed Mythos
			
Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
			
Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
			
Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable

When they launched on June 9th, Anthropic said “Fable 5’s capabilities exceed those of any […]

title: (No AI) Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
author: Robert Hart
content:
![An image showing Donald Trump on a red and green background](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/04/VRG_Illo_STK175_L_Normand_DonaldTrump_Negative.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

At Washington's request, Anthropic [suddenly took its newest and most powerful AI models offline](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949553/anthropic-fable-5-mythos-5-government-national-security) over the weekend. The American company [said](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access) it had little choice after the White House demanded it block access for all foreign nationals, including its own employees. Abroad, the incident offered a sobering reminder that the US not only dominates frontier AI - its government also wields power over who gets to use it.

The Trump administration's action was swift, sweeping, and imposed with little warning or explanation. The unprecedented shutdown of the [Fable 5 and Mythos 5](https://www.theverge.com/news/946725/anthropic-releases-claude-fable-5-mythos) models - which were already subject to [safeguards limiting their use](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/947973/fable-wont-answer-basic-biology-questions) in " …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949986/anthropic-fable-mythos-shutdown-sovereign-ai)

The American company said it had little choice after the White House demanded it block access for all foreign nationals, including its own employees. Abroad, the incident offered a sobering reminder that the US not only dominates frontier AI - its government also wields power over who gets to use it. 
The Trump administration's action was swift, sweeping, and imposed with little warning or explanation. The unprecedented shutdown of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models - which were already subject to safeguards limiting their use in " …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
content: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.
contentSnippet: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.

title: (No AI) New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
content: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.
contentSnippet: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.

title: (No AI) Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
content: Google DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research.
contentSnippet: Google DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research.

creator: Dell Cameron, Dhruv Mehrotra
title: (No AI) Meta Tapped a Pentagon Supplier to Prototype Face Recognition for Its Glasses
dc:creator: Dell Cameron, Dhruv Mehrotra
content: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.
contentSnippet: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.

creator: Fernanda González
title: (No AI) A Court Has Ruled That Google Is Liable for False Statements Generated by AI Overviews
dc:creator: Fernanda González
content: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.
contentSnippet: The ruling holds that a company that designs, trains, operates, and manages an AI system must assume legal liability for any damages caused by the responses it generates.
